findings: Species identity is correct: the plate depicts an Eastern Phoebe, Sayornis phoebe, family Tyrannidae.

All displayed measurements match Cornell's published ranges: length 14–17 cm (5.5–6.7 in), weight 16–21 g (0.6–0.7 oz), and wingspan 26–28 cm (10.2–11.0 in). Audubon publishes closely overlapping ranges.

The written field marks are accurate and appropriately nuanced: large often-peaked head, darker sooty head and pale throat, plain gray-brown upperparts, weak or absent adult wingbars, pale underparts that may show yellow, short dark bill, and medium-length roughly square tail.

The illustration agrees well with the reference photographs in overall shape, upright posture, dark head, pale throat and belly, plain wings, short dark bill, dark eye without an obvious eye-ring, and long dark tail.

The main bird has one head, one beak, two legs, one tail, and normal paired-wing anatomy; the far wing is naturally hidden by the side-profile pose. The separate lower head and feather are clearly supplemental studies, not duplicated anatomy on the main bird.

Minor anatomical limitation: the gripping toes are crowded and somewhat loop-like, reducing clarity of normal anisodactyl foot structure. The eye is also slightly oversized and unusually glossy for a scientific illustration, but neither issue materially affects identification.
Typography is legible, correctly spelled, and consistently arranged. The unlabeled tick scale is decorative rather than quantitatively interpretable, but it makes no false numerical claim.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Eastern Phoebe Identification — All About Birds
